mnemonics: KWILL - K-Weighted-Instance Learning: K neighbors, Weights by inverse distance, Instance-based, Lazy learner, Local approximation
keyPoints: Assigns weights to neighbors based on distance (closer neighbors have more influence)
Uses inverse distance (1/d) or squared inverse distance (1/d²) for weighting
Reduces impact of noisy neighbors compared to standard K-NN
commonMistakes: Forgetting to normalize features before calculating distances (causing bias toward features with larger scales)
Using inappropriate distance metrics for the data type (e.g., Euclidean for categorical data)
Setting K too small (overfitting to noise) or too large (underfitting local patterns)
